title: Compressed Sensing (random-projection linear inverse)
sub_domain: Sparse linear inverse under RIP measurement
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Donoho 2006 / Candes-Tao 2006 CS foundations; Tropp-Gilbert 2007 OMP; Daubechies et al. 2004 ISTA/FISTA; Metzler-Maleki-Baraniuk 2016 LDAMP.

E.description: A sparse (or compressible in some basis Psi) signal x is measured via a sub-sampling sensing matrix A in R^{M x N} with M << N; the RIP condition on A allows exact recovery of k-sparse x with overwhelming probability when M = O(k log(N/k)).
E.forward_model: y = A * x + n;   A in R^{M x N}, M/N in [0.1, 0.8]
E.world_state_x: Signal x in R^N, k-sparse in basis Psi (|supp(Psi^T x)| = k)
E.observation_y: Measurement y in R^M (Gaussian or sub-Gaussian noise)
physical_parameters_theta: Sensing matrix type: Gaussian, Bernoulli, partial-DFT, sub-Gaussian

W.regime: Well-posed under RIP (Candes-Tao 2006); kappa = (1+delta_2k)/(1-delta_2k). Failure when measurement_ratio < 2*k/N * log(N/k). Mismatch parameters: sparsity_overestimation, non-exact-sparsity (compressible).

C.solver_class: L1-min (LASSO, BP, ISTA/FISTA), greedy (OMP, CoSaMP, SP), AMP, learned (LDAMP, LISTA, ISTA-Net+)
C.error_bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_n * sqrt(k*log(N)/M) + C2 * ||x - x_k||_1 / sqrt(k); tight for random Gaussian A
C.complexity: OMP: O(M*N*k); LASSO/FISTA: O(M*N*iter); AMP: O(M*N*iter) with fastest convergence
